projects.name: HiveOps

bullets.text: Shipped an agentic AI platform where specialized LLM agents collaborate on incident triage, root-cause analysis, remediation, and verification, with human-in-the-loop approval between steps.

Built an async FastAPI backend that coordinates the agents and runs proposed fixes in a sandboxed simulation before applying them. Stored past incidents in Supabase and pulled relevant prior cases as context for each new incident, keeping agent prompts bounded.

Built the React dashboard with sub-task tracking, audit logs, and live agent-state updates streamed via Supabase Realtime. Added an incident chat where the LLM has live access to the incident's current state.

projects.credibility_anchor: Won Best Use of Hex API at YHack Spring 2026

projects.name: PRGenie

bullets.text: Built a GitHub-native AI agent that scores contributor trust, estimates change risk on incoming pull requests, suggests reviewers, and flags high-demand issues for maintainers to triage.

Built a seven-stage agent pipeline (trust scoring, risk analysis, triage, reviewer routing, persona extraction, review commenting, issue-demand detection) where each stage wrote its outputs to a shared SQLite store so later stages could read prior results without re-hitting the GitHub API.
Integrated NemoClaw policy enforcement so every label, comment, and review action is checked against repository-level safety rules before execution.

projects.credibility_anchor: Won Deep Tech track at Red Hat Open Accelerator Hackathon 2026

projects.name: FoodGrid: Boston

bullets.text: Built a civic web app with a 4-person team: a React map UI (Resident and Government modes, neighborhood-level metrics) backed by Django REST APIs serving census-tract GeoJSON, resource filters, and supply simulation endpoints.
Integrated a Node/Express chatbot grounded on the app's own data using Llama 3.1, with multilingual prompt handling so non-English-speaking residents could query the map in their language.

projects.credibility_anchor: Won Best Overall and Best EcoHack track at MLH Civic Hacks 2026

bullets.text: Built backend SQL queries and REST APIs for Real Estate CRM and HRMS modules, shipping features that were deployed to 4 enterprise clients.
Optimized SQL queries and backend APIs to improve throughput by 15% and cut page load time by 1.2s on the most-used flows.
Tracked down recurring data inconsistencies across 6 modules and fixed them at the source, reducing error tickets by roughly 25%.
Researched EV route planning under Dr. Naveen Kumar using a Fuzzy Genetic Algorithm to optimize charging-station selection along multi-stop long-distance routes.
Built the routing simulator in Python and benchmarked across 10 solution batches: 26% lower charging cost and 3% lower total trip time versus greedy baselines.
Presented at CTSEM 2025, MANIT Bhopal; in press, Lecture Notes in Civil Engineering, Vol. 871, Springer Nature (July 2026).

publications.title: Optimizing Transportation Cost in Long-Distance Electric Vehicle Using Fuzzy Genetic Algorithm
publications.venue: Lecture Notes in Civil Engineering, Vol. 871, Springer Nature
